commands.review_summary:
Adds opt-in per-agent iteration budgets for embedded agent tool-calling rounds, with core pre-tool-round enforcement, OpenClaw/Codex harness support, budget-exhausted results, docs, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +580, Tests +1208, Docs +44. Total +1832 across 37 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/proof level: current main lacks a hard pre-tool budget, #9912 documents the requested loop cap, and the PR body shows a configured runaway local provider stopped by the budget.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is maintainer review of public config/API/harness semantics.

target.title: feat(agents): add iteration budget for agent loop safety
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw has heuristic loop detection and whole-run timeouts, but no explicit per-agent hard budget for LLM tool-calling rounds. Production reports show that some agent/model/channel combinations can keep calling tools after useful results are available, duplicate visible messages, or continue consuming model calls after lifecycle bugs.

This PR adds an opt-in circuit breaker for those cases. It is intentionally disabled by default and configurable per agent so long-horizon agents can keep larger budgets while high-risk chat/tool agents can be bounded.

Addresses: #6890, #75468, #89090, #79301, #75355, #77625, #74859, #82518

## Why This Change Was Made

The implementation adds:

- `agents.defaults.iterationBudget` and per-agent `agents.list[].iterationBudget` overrides.
- `enabled`, `maxIterations`, `subagentMaxIterations`, and `forceSummaryOnExhaustion` config fields.
- An `onBeforeToolCallingRound` agent-core hook that runs after a model emits tool calls but before those tools execute.
- Embedded-runner budget exhaustion handling that returns `budget_exhausted`.
- Harness opt-in via `supportsIterationBudget`, with built-in OpenClaw and Codex support.
- Fail-closed behavior when a selected harness does not support budget enforcement.

## User Impact

Default behavior is unchanged because the budget is disabled unless configured. When enabled, the budget caps LLM tool-calling rounds, not individual tool calls and not total long-horizon task duration. A single model response containing multiple tool calls counts as one round.

Operators can use this as a safety net for agents where runaway tool loops are more harmful than stopping early. Long-horizon agents should either leave the budget disabled or set an intentionally higher per-agent limit.

## Evidence

### Real configured OpenClaw run proof

Ran a real local OpenClaw CLI agent turn through `openclaw agent --local --json`
with an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and a
temporary workspace. The config selected an OpenAI-compatible local provider and
enabled:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "iterationBudget": {
        "enabled": true,
        "maxIterations": 2,
        "forceSummaryOnExhaustion": false
      }
    }
  }
}
```

The local provider intentionally behaved like a runaway model: every streamed
model response requested another `read` tool call. The run used normal OpenClaw
tool projection and the built-in `read` tool.

Redacted provider request log:

```json
[
  { "request": 1, "stream": true, "messageCount": 2, "toolResultCount": 0, "selectedTool": "read" },
  { "request": 2, "stream": true, "messageCount": 4, "toolResultCount": 1, "selectedTool": "read" },
  { "request": 3, "stream": true, "messageCount": 6, "toolResultCount": 2, "selectedTool": "read" }
]
```

Redacted terminal/log proof:

```text
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=localbudget api=openai-completions model=budget-loop-model method=POST url=http://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1/chat/completions timeoutMs=30000 proxy=none policy=custom
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=localbudget api=openai-completions model=budget-loop-model status=200 elapsedMs=17 contentType=text/event-stream
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=localbudget api=openai-completions model=budget-loop-model method=POST url=http://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1/chat/completions timeoutMs=30000 proxy=none policy=custom
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=localbudget api=openai-completions model=budget-loop-model status=200 elapsedMs=4 contentType=text/event-stream
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=localbudget api=openai-completions model=budget-loop-model method=POST url=http://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1/chat/completions timeoutMs=30000 proxy=none policy=custom
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=localbudget api=openai-completions model=budget-loop-model status=200 elapsedMs=4 contentType=text/event-stream
[agent/embedded] iteration budget exhausted: runId=<redacted> sessionId=<redacted> used=2 max=2
```

CLI JSON result:

```json
{
  "payloadText": "Iteration budget exhausted (2/2). The agent exceeded its iteration budget.",
  "errorKind": "budget_exhausted",
  "errorMessage": "Iteration budget exhausted after 2 iterations (max: 2).",
  "livenessState": "blocked",
  "replayInvalid": true
}
```

### Real embedded-runner proof

Added `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.iteration-budget.test.ts` to exercise the runner-level contract:

- configures `agents.defaults.iterationBudget.enabled: true`
- sets `maxIterations: 2`
- drives three tool-calling rounds through the embedded attempt callback
- proves rounds 1 and 2 are allowed and round 3 is blocked
- asserts the outer runner returns `budget_exhausted`
- asserts the terminal state is `blocked` and replay is invalidated

### Crabbox/Testbox proof

Provider: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.

Final proof run:

- Testbox id: `tbx_01kwcjpdrdg1677bw02j5aybjh`
- Actions run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28456447112
- Result: exit `0`
- Summary: 4 Vitest shards passed in `30.90s`; total run `4m12.521s`

Command:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.iteration-budget.test.ts \
  packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts \
  src/agents/iteration-budget.test.ts \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/budget-exhaustion.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.dynamic-tools.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.native-hook-relay.test.ts
```

Covered:

- runner-level configured budget exhaustion
- core `onBeforeToolCallingRound` behavior before tool execution
- budget result construction
- harness fail-closed behavior for unsupported harnesses
- built-in OpenClaw harness budget support
- Codex native hook relay and dynamic tool budget enforcement

Earlier focused Testbox proof before the final runner proof test was added:

- Testbox id: `tbx_01kwcbrts7d1ct7g2n1df9tdtz`
- Actions run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28448345979
- Result: exit `0`
- Summary: focused budget/harness/Codex coverage passed in `34.13s`; total run `1m37.618s`

### Local closeout

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.iteration-budget.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` focused budget/harness suite
- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`
- autoreview clean on the final test commit
